title: Record issue events to per-event JSON files
description:
As a backend engineer, I want every issue create/update/delete/state-change/comment action to emit a single JSON event file in project/events/ named with the ISO8601 timestamp, so that the system has an append-only, time-ordered log without relying on filesystem mtimes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Event types at minimum: issue_created, state_transition, comment_added, field_updated, issue_deleted.
- Each file contains: event_id, issue_id, event_type, occurred_at (ISO8601), actor_id, and a payload specific to the type (e.g., from_state/to_state; comment_id; changed_fields).
- Exactly one file per event; no batching; no alternate storage.
creator: ryan

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation. Focus first on event model/helpers + event writing in Rust/Python for issue creation/update/comment/delete/deps/transfer and console API+UI tab. Will keep event writes strict (rollback on failure) and use RFC3339+UUID filenames under project/events or project-local/events.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Completed: issue create/update/comment/delete/dependency/transfer/beads now emit per-event JSON files (RFC3339+UUID) with strict rollback on write failure. Events land in project/events or project-local/events based on issue location.
